# B011. Bayesian Hierarchical Fundamental Model

## Positioning

This candidate pushes slower-moving fundamentals through a hierarchical prior so sector, industry, and asset level signals can coexist. It is a natural fit for monthly or weekly rebalance schedules rather than for intraday trading.

## Reference Basis

- [View fusion vis-à-vis a Bayesian interpretation of Black-Litterman for portfolio allocation](../../references/papers/2301.13594_black_litterman_fusion.pdf) - posterior fusion of views across hierarchy levels
- [Multivariate Bayesian Structural Time Series Model](../../references/papers/1801.03222_multivariate_bsts.pdf) - latent structural trend and macro framing

## Repo Anchors

### Operational Guardrails

Shared safety and execution rails used by every candidate before a signal can touch capital.
- [src/risk/manager.py](../../../src/risk/manager.py) - pre-trade risk gate and position haircut
- [src/brokers/paper.py](../../../src/brokers/paper.py) - paper-safe broker adapter
- [src/monitoring/health.py](../../../src/monitoring/health.py) - freshness, heartbeat, and kill-switch checks
- [src/backtest/engine.py](../../../src/backtest/engine.py) - deterministic offline backtest runner
- [src/backtest/matching_engine.py](../../../src/backtest/matching_engine.py) - queue-aware synthetic fill model
- [scripts/monitor_health.py](../../../scripts/monitor_health.py) - runtime health loop and stale-data monitor

### Fundamental Stack

Point-in-time fundamentals and macro-aware feature joins for slower rebalance cycles.
- [src/ingestion/loader.py](../../../src/ingestion/loader.py) - fundamental and macro data loader
- [src/features/builder.py](../../../src/features/builder.py) - feature joins and lag alignment
- [src/models/lgbm.py](../../../src/models/lgbm.py) - tabular score model for the hybrid stack

## Data / Feature Bundle

- Use point-in-time fundamentals, sector labels, and macro series from the shared loader path.
- Lag all reporting variables to their true availability date before they reach the feature builder.
- Blend fundamentals with technical and regime features only after lag alignment is locked down.

## Execution Path

- Infer sector and asset-level posterior scores and turn them into a rebalance list.
- Use a constrained allocation rule so the hierarchy remains readable and bounded.
- Run the rebalance only on the configured schedule; this is not a high-turnover signal.

## Risk Controls

- Reject any input that violates point-in-time discipline or report-date lag.
- Avoid concentration if a single sector dominates the posterior hierarchy.
- Keep the model inactive when the reporting calendar makes the data stale.

## Validation Gates

- Compare against the fundamental-heavy hybrids F012 and F025.
- Test the rebalance schedule on a monthly and a weekly cadence to ensure stability.
- Check that the hierarchy improves risk-adjusted allocation rather than simply overfitting sectors.

## Archive Triggers

- Archive if point-in-time alignment is too fragile to maintain.
- Archive if the hierarchy is less stable than a simpler BL or HRP overlay.
- Archive if the gain is not visible after costs and rebalance delay.

## Next Step

Use it as the slow-moving fundamental benchmark for F012 and F025.
